workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days? List subject patterns, sender/recipient groups, and approximate time spent.
Are there recurring email types I write regularly - like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests? Show examples from the past week.
Which emails required the most back-and-forth (most replies in a thread) this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type (1:1, team sync, external, etc.)?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats - things people regularly come to me for?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations this week?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week? Any recurring doc types like reports, specs, or decks?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence (weekly/monthly reports, trackers, dashboards)?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time this week?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat - where I do the same sequence of actions (e.g., get email -> schedule meeting -> create doc)?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: Dozens of distinct ADO emails across Tasks, Bugs, Requirements, Epics, and Features appear throughout the week. Dominant category among 199 total emails. Low per-email depth, high cumulative scanning time.

signals.title: Evals and CAPE Project Email Thread Participation
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject patterns: Customer donated evals added Playbook, CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive, Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites, EvalCon Register/propose content. Multiple multi-reply multi-recipient threads with long bodies and attachments. Moderate to high per-thread cognitive load.

signals.title: Compliance and Security Alert Handling
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject pattern: Action Required: File Blocked - take action to protect sensitive data. Sender: no-reply@sharepointonline.com / Microsoft Purview. Multiple alerts on the same day related to different files. Short but interruptive - requires action or confirmation.

signals.title: Company-Wide Digest and Broadcast Review
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject patterns: Microsoft Daily Digest, Microsoft Weekly Digest, Viva Engage announcements (Copilot, Immigration Q&A, Champs). Senders: Viva Engage / Employee Comms / Microsoft Digital. Often skimmed or skipped unless headline-relevant.

signals.title: Short Acknowledgement and Courtesy Reply Writing
signals.rawExcerpt: Pattern: 1-3 sentences, gratitude plus confirmation, no new requests. Consistent relationship-maintenance style: warm, concise, and timely. Example: brief thank-you reply closing a mentorship loop.

signals.title: In-Thread Status Update Contributions
signals.rawExcerpt: Pattern: Reply within an existing thread, summarizes progress or alignment, often references a prior sync. Example: I synced with colleague yesterday regarding training content and timeline. We agreed to aim for early next week. PM-style update: factual, decision-oriented, and scoped.

signals.title: Urgent Multi-Party Escalation Thread Management
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread: URGENT: DBS - Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites. Explicit additions of more people mid-thread, typical of fast-moving reply chains. Multiple rapid replies and escalations in the same thread.

signals.title: External Customer Follow-Up Email Loop
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread: [EXTERNAL] Expeditors + MSFT screen share. External customer thread with follow-ups after a live meeting. Includes attachments. Multi-day cadence: meeting to follow-up to responses.

signals.title: Email to Meeting to Shared Doc Repeating Workflow
signals.rawExcerpt: Most common repeating workflow sequence. Step 1: Someone emails a document or idea. Step 2: Meeting joined or organized to discuss it. Step 3: Outcomes land in a Loop doc or shared file. This pattern appears across PM reviews, eval work, and cross-team coordination.

signals.title: Weekly 1:1 with Manager
signals.rawExcerpt: Recurring 1:1 (25 min) clustered on Monday. Involves substantive back-and-forth around EvalCon, eval strategy, customer feedback, and roadmap alignment.

signals.title: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Meeting (25 min, ~19 attendees). Every Monday ~8:05-8:30 AM. Same v-team, same organizer, same weekly time. Evals strategy, customer adoption, tooling gaps, and ownership across CAPE.

signals.title: CAPE Architecture Call
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Architecture Call (55 min, 200+ attendees). Every Monday ~9:05-10:00 AM. Declarative vs custom agents, orchestration, convergence roadmap, tooling differences. Very stable weekly time and org-wide CAPE audience.

signals.title: US Pod Stand Up
signals.rawExcerpt: US Pod Stand Up (25 min). Every Tuesday ~12:35-1:00 PM. Consistent core attendees and fixed weekly slot. Part of heavy Monday and Tuesday meeting concentration.

signals.title: CAPE Customer Engagement Weekly Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE / Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync. Every Friday ~10:00-11:00 AM. Same core group of PMs and engagement leads week over week.

signals.title: BizChat Offline Evaluation Office Hour
signals.rawExcerpt: [US] BizChat Offline Evaluation Office Hour. Every Wednesday ~5:00-5:30 PM. Office hours with large audience but very stable hosts and schedule.

signals.title: Weekly Office Hours for Deploying Agents and Apps
signals.rawExcerpt: Office Hours: Deploying Agents and Apps to Microsoft. Every Thursday ~11:00-11:25 AM. Same CAT + CAPE participants repeatedly.

signals.title: Learning and Scaling Fridays Standing Session
signals.rawExcerpt: Learning and Scaling Fridays - PST. Every Friday ~2:05-5:00 PM. Small, stable group. Strong signal of a standing team ritual.

signals.title: CAPE Camp AIR Multi-Day Program Sessions
signals.rawExcerpt: Full-day Camp AIR blocks on Monday and Tuesday. Multiple sequential sessions: AI-first mindset, workflow analysis, agent creation and publishing, vibe coding, Power Apps, Dataverse, Copilot Studio architecture. Largest contiguous time blocks this week.

signals.title: MCS Analytics and Evaluations PM Review
signals.rawExcerpt: MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review (recurring forum). Loop doc with agenda, decisions, open questions, and meeting notes tied to product reviews and stakeholder discussions.

signals.title: Repeated Eval Guidance Requests from Colleagues
signals.rawExcerpt: People regularly come for practical, opinionated guidance on how to do evals well. Typical questions: What eval method should I use? How many test cases is enough? What is a realistic pass rate? When should evals happen in the agent lifecycle? Repeated discussions about eval-driven development vs MVP-first approaches.

signals.title: Customer Eval Support and Deep-Dive Requests
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple requests to join customer calls specifically for eval guidance. Being asked to sanity-check eval sets, templates, or run live demos for customers. Signal: seen as the person who can de-risk evals in front of customers.

signals.title: Eval Tool Bug and Behavior Debugging
signals.rawExcerpt: People ask to help diagnose confusing or broken eval behavior. Recurring themes: CSV export/import losing settings, adaptive cards not evaluating correctly, inconsistent results across runs, graders behaving differently than expected.

signals.title: EvalCon Planning and Content Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: EvalCon planning shows up repeatedly: whether and what CAPE should present, framing content around blockers, challenges, and process gaps, submission deadlines and coordination with v-teams.

signals.title: Technical Artifact and Repo Sharing
signals.rawExcerpt: Notable pattern: sharing GitHub repos (agent eval scenarios, skills, tools), sharing PowerPoint decks and Loop pages, providing aka.ms links for reuse. Shows up across many different chats rather than being isolated to one thread.

signals.title: Product Feedback Routing and VoC Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeated asks: Where should eval-related feedback go? Is there a forum or cadence to review VoCs? How do we get eval learnings in front of the PG? Who owns what between PM, TPM, and field? Questions about routing feature requests, ADO epics, and ownership.

signals.title: Copilot Studio Tooling Gap Discussions
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeated discussion of Copilot Studio eval capabilities, missing features (permissions, automation, environment movement), CLI tools and plugins, skill detection/creation. These topics recur across chats with engineers, PMs, and field teams.

signals.title: JSON Config and Agent State File Authoring
signals.rawExcerpt: Most frequently appearing file type is .json. Includes: configuration/state files, task and workflow artifacts, session, workspace, and debug outputs. Many authored and modified repeatedly across the week, often clustered on the same days.

signals.title: Loop Meeting Notes and PM Review Doc Authoring
signals.rawExcerpt: Loop-based meeting and review notes especially for product reviews and PM syncs. Example: MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review - a Loop doc with agenda, decisions, open questions. Recurring habit of using Loop for PM reviews, design feedback, and cross-team alignment.

signals.title: Skilluminator Cycle Dashboard Weekly Update
signals.rawExcerpt: Skilluminator Cycle Dashboard (dashboard.html). Explicit cycle labeling (Cycle 3, Cycle 2). Generated Week of 2026-03-16 language. Comparative metrics across cycles (growth %, signals harvested, trends). Clearest example of a systematic, repeatable update loop.

signals.title: Agent Eval 4-Week Workplan Tracker Updates
signals.rawExcerpt: Agent_Eval_4Week_Workplan.docx. Explicit 4-week structure. Deliverables with week-based due dates (End of Wk 3, End of Wk 4). Status fields intended for ongoing updates. Functions as a program tracker, not a static plan.

signals.title: Daily Planning Page Creation
signals.rawExcerpt: File: [Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day. Date-stamped, structured as a reusable daily template. Likely cadence: daily. Not a team report, but clearly a repeated personal workflow.

signals.title: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Proliferation
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple people creating and sharing separate eval-related decks: customer intro slides, pitch decks, enablement decks, summit decks. Team explicitly calls out multiple versions floating and confusion for PMs and the field.

signals.title: SDR Discovery Documentation Format Duplication
signals.rawExcerpt: Different team members independently creating: Word-based SDRs, PowerPoint discovery decks, simplified SDR templates, meeting-to-SDR conversion workflows. Explicit discussions about whether there should be one SDR format vs multiple.
